libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Configure generated tests to pass the same module opens on JVM tests and native-image builds: --add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED, --add-opens=java.base/java.net=ALL-UNNAMED, and --add-opens=java.base/sun.nio.ch=ALL-UNNAMED. Tests should explicitly initialize Conscrypt via Conscrypt.checkAvailability() and Conscrypt.newProvider(), and can exercise TLS/SSLEngine/socket behavior with loopback-only resources; no external server is required.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8879 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.conscrypt:conscrypt-openjdk-uber:1.4.1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Conscrypt 1.4.1 predates strong JDK encapsulation and uses reflection on Socket, SocketImpl, SocketChannel, InetAddress, and ECParameterSpec internals; missing opens can make otherwise meaningful tests fail before metadata coverage is reached.
The artifact loads JNI libraries from META-INF/native, so native-image verification remains authoritative for resource/native-library metadata.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed: the uber artifact has no POM dependencies and bundles the native Conscrypt libraries. Meaningful coverage does require JDK module-opening JVM/native-image arguments because Conscrypt 1.4.1 reflects into JDK internals.
